intro-1.plaintext: Container blocks hold other blocks inside them — sliders with slides, grids with columns, accordions with panels. Define them in your blockSchema using blocks_layout or object_list widgets.

children.text: Container blocks hold other blocks inside them — sliders with slides, grids with columns, accordions with panels. Define them in your blockSchema using

empty-text.plaintext: A container can never be empty. When the last child is deleted, a defaultBlockType or empty block is inserted.

children.text: A container can never be empty. When the last child is deleted, either the
is added, or a special block with
is inserted. Empty blocks are stripped before saving. Render them as empty space — Hydra puts a '+' button in the middle for the user to replace it.

bl-text.plaintext: Each child has its own @type and schema (from blocks). Children are stored in a shared  dict on the parent, with the field holding  for ordering:

tabs.label: JavaScript

bl-text-t2.plaintext: All blocks_layout fields on the same block share the same blocks dict. So a block can have multiple container fields (e.g., header_blocks and footer_blocks) whose children all live in the parent's blocks.

children.text: All blocks_layout fields on the same block share the same blocks dict. So a block can have multiple container fields (e.g., header_blocks and footer_blocks) whose children all live in the parent's blocks.

ol-text.plaintext: All items share one inline schema, stored as an array with an ID field. Use  when the data is nested within the block:

typed-ol-text.plaintext: When  is set on an object_list, items can have different types (like blocks_layout) but are still stored as an array. Each item's type is stored in the field specified by  (defaults to '@type') and its schema is looked up from blocks:

typed-ol-text-t2.plaintext: Both blocks_layout and object_list look the same in the editing UI and blocks can be dragged between them — data is automatically adapted when moving between formats (ID fields added/stripped, type fields set appropriately).

children.text: Both blocks_layout and object_list look the same in the editing UI and blocks can be dragged between them — data is automatically adapted when moving between formats (ID fields added/stripped, type fields set appropriately).

children.text: — Controls where the '+' button appears. By default it will be the opposite of its parent. Use "bottom" for vertical stacking, "right" for horizontal.

inherit-heading.plaintext: Synchronised Block Types in a Container

children.text: Synchronised Block Types in a Container

inherit-text-t0.plaintext: You might want one container type that holds different block types but constrains them to all be the same type with synchronised settings. A field on the parent lets the editor select the type and all blocks get converted using fieldMappings:

children.text: You might want one container type that holds different block types but constrains them to all be the same type with synchronised settings. A field on the parent lets the editor select the type and all blocks get converted using fieldMappings:

tabs.label: JavaScript

inherit-text-t2.plaintext: inheritSchemaFrom: Parent inherits schema from selected child type. When the type field changes, child blocks sync to new type.typeField: Field name for selecting child type (e.g., 'variation')defaultsField: Field name for storing inherited defaults (e.g., 'itemDefaults')blocksField: Which blocks field the sub-blocks are in. Set to ".." to use the parent's allowedBlocks.filterConvertibleFrom: Only allow selecting a block type which can convert from the specified type.childBlockConfig: Child hides fields except editableFields when inside a parent with inheritSchemaFrom.

children.text: : Parent inherits schema from selected child type. When the type field changes, child blocks sync to new type.

: Field name for storing inherited defaults (e.g., 'itemDefaults')
: Which blocks field the sub-blocks are in. Set to ".." to use the parent's allowedBlocks.
: Only allow selecting a block type which can convert from the specified type.
: Child hides fields except editableFields when inside a parent with inheritSchemaFrom.
